Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible impaired kidney function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate renal or urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, possibly reflecting anemia or recent hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible underlying causes for abnormal urine findings.
